title: Why Oportun Financial Corporation's (OPRT) Stock Is Up 13.67%
text: As of May 15, 2026, OPRT trades at $5.25, down 2.42% from the previous close. P/E Ratio: 14.2 (higher than the Consumer Finance industry median of 8.2). Value Grade: A (considered Deep Value). Momentum Score: 30 (Weak momentum). 52-Week Range: $4.03 to $7.97. Year-to-Date Performance: Down 0.76%. According to analysis, 2 analysts upgraded over the last month while none downgraded OPRT during this period. However, this recent upgrade activity hasn't translated into sustained price momentum. The article emphasizes that investors should avoid relying solely on price movements. AAII recommends evaluating stocks through a variety of metrics, fundamentals, and analytics rather than focusing on recent price changes or historical returns alone. The company's relative weakness in momentum suggests cautious positioning despite its deep value designation.
